items.briefing:
Australian small and medium businesses are increasingly adopting AI tools at work, but many are doing so without proper oversight or updated workflows — creating "shadow AI" risks where staff use unapproved tools that could expose sensitive data. Organisations are urged to establish clear policies around which AI tools employees can use and how.

In positive news, the Netherlands recently seized 800 servers and arrested two individuals linked to cybercriminal activity, disrupting infrastructure used to support attacks globally.

**Practical tip:** Ask your IT team whether your organisation has an approved list of AI tools — if there isn't one, it's time to create one.

featured.title: Avoiding shadow AI requires strong enterprise governance
featured.quote: Unmanaged AI use is exposing Australian firms to data leakage and compliance breaches.
